behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and continue through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the behavior explicitly spans parser output handing off into normal hook input handling, so a single-function unit test is too narrow. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require real process/stdin execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test wiring the real parser + hook input handler. Feed valid JSON whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and $vars. Assert parse succeeds and the normal handling path is reached with expected structured fields.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads emit a json_parse_failed trace record with diagnosable malformed-section context.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser + trace-record construction/propagation, crossing module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require real stdin/process. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because no explicit real process/OS condition appears in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test with real parser and trace emitter path. Provide malformed JSON cases (unterminated string, missing comma, extra closing brace). Assert json_parse_failed is emitted and includes location/context fields sufficient to identify malformed section.

behaviors.description: Local error-policy maps parse failure into a handled (non-throwing) result and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says local error-policy path; this is a local mapping contract (error input -> handled result + telemetry intent flag) without required module wiring or real infra. Rejected Integration because no explicit cross-module handoff is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy function/object directly with a parse-failure error value. Assert it returns handled status (no throw) and sets telemetry intent metadata/flag as expected.

behaviors.description: Under real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires real process execution, real stdin, and exit behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss crash/exit-order issues. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the real hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed JSON to stdin, and observe process exit + emitted trace output. Assert no crash (controlled exit behavior) and trace signal appears before process termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) emits expected trace artifact on malformed input.
behaviors.justification: EVIDENCE-ANCHOR: behavior text explicitly names a real invocation path starting from stdin and going through parser to trace sink, which is real process/IO boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked stdin/sink can miss wiring and emission semantics. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real invocation entrypoint, pass malformed stdin payload, and collect the real trace artifact output/store. Assert artifact type/content matches expected parse-failure trace contract.

behaviors.description: Regression coverage differentiates malformed-JSON failures from valid markdown-heavy payloads to avoid blaming markdown content.
behaviors.justification: This is a comparative behavior across two input classes through the hook input pipeline (success path vs parse-failure path), which is broader than a single parser helper and benefits from module wiring. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real process/stdin; distinction can be validated in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterized in-process regression test using real parser + handling/trace path: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings should parse and not emit parse-failure trace; (b) malformed JSON should emit json_parse_failed. Assert outcomes are cleanly separated.
